perturbation.original_text: Diplomat agrees to pay KI, Inc. as royalty a sum equal to % of the net wholesale volume of the products covered by this Agreement by Diplomat and its affiliated, associated, or subsidiary companies. The term "net wholesale volume" shall mean gross sales to all customers; less returns, trade discounts and cash discounts; but no deduction shall be made for other discounts or uncollectible accounts. No costs incurred in the manufacture, sale, distribution, or exploitation of the products covered by this Agreement shall be deducted from any royalty payable by Diplomat.
perturbation.changed_text: Diplomat agrees to pay KI, Inc. as royalty a sum equal to % of the net wholesale volume of the products covered by this Agreement by Diplomat and its affiliated, associated, or subsidiary companies. The term "net wholesale volume" shall mean gross sales to all customers; less returns, trade discounts and cash discounts; deductions can be made for other discounts or uncollectible accounts, with documented approval from Diplomat's CFO. No costs incurred in the manufacture, sale, distribution, or exploitation of the products covered by this Agreement shall be deducted from any royalty payable by Diplomat.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction in the definition of 'net wholesale volume.' Originally, 'no deduction shall be made for other discounts or uncollectible accounts.' The modified text now allows deductions for these items with CFO approval. This conflicts with the original strict definition and creates ambiguity in royalty calculation. This creates an in-text contradiction since the original definition states a clear rule, and now a new rule is made that contradicts the previous one, because it will not be clear at time of royalty payments to see which definition is being used.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Diplomat agrees to pay KI, Inc. as royalty a sum equal to % of the net wholesale volume of the products covered by this Agreement by Diplomat and its affiliated, associated, or subsidiary companies. The term "net wholesale volume" shall mean gross sales to all customers; less returns, trade discounts and cash discounts; deductions can be made for other discounts or uncollectible accounts, with documented approval from Diplomat's CFO. No costs incurred in the manufacture, sale, distribution, or exploitation of the products covered by this Agreement shall be deducted from any royalty payable by Diplomat.
perturbation.justification: The modified text directly contradicts the original definition of 'net wholesale volume.' The original text explicitly states that 'no deduction shall be made for other discounts or uncollectible accounts,' while the changed text allows such deductions with CFO approval. This creates a clear conflict in the terms of royalty calculation, as it introduces uncertainty about which definition applies when determining the royalty payable. Therefore, it introduces ambiguity in the definition, making enforcement difficult or interpretation unclear.

perturbation.original_text: Diplomat agrees that the products covered by this Agreement shall be of such style, appearance and quality as to be adequate and suited to their exploitation to the best advantage, protection and enhancement of KI's name and the good will pertaining to such name. Diplomat further agrees that (a) such products will be manufactured, sold and distributed in accordance with all applicable Federal, State and local laws, (b) that the policy of sale, distribution, and/or exploitation by Diplomat shall be to the best advantage of KI, Inc. and KI, and (c) that the latter policy shall in no manner reflect adversely upon the good name of KI and KI, Inc. To this end, Diplomat shall, before selling or distributing any of the products, furnish to KI, Inc. for its approval, free of cost, a reasonable number of samples of each product and the cartons, containers, packing and wrapping material for such products. The quality and style of such products, as well as of any carton, container or packing or wrapping material, shall be subject to the approval of KI, Inc., which shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed. Failure to reject any product, carton, container, or packing or wrapping within 15 days of receipt of such item or items by KI, Inc. shall be deemed an acceptance of the quality and style of such item or items. After samples have been approved pursuant to this paragraph, Diplomat shall not depart therefrom in any material respect without KI, Inc.'s prior written consent. From time to time after Diplomat has commenced selling the products, and upon KI, Inc.'s written request, Diplomat shall furnish without cost to KI, Inc. not more than ten (10) additional random samples of each product being manufactured and sold by Diplomat under this Agreement, together with any containers and packing and wrapping material used in connection with such products.
perturbation.changed_text: Diplomat agrees that the products covered by this Agreement should generally align with a style, appearance, and quality suitable for exploiting KI's name. Diplomat agrees that (a) such products will generally be manufactured, sold, and distributed in accordance with applicable laws, (b) that the policy of sale, distribution, and/or exploitation by Diplomat should be generally to the best advantage of KI, Inc. and KI, and (c) that the latter policy should generally not reflect adversely upon the good name of KI and KI, Inc. To this end, while not mandatory, Diplomat may furnish samples of each product and the cartons, containers, packing and wrapping material for such products to KI, Inc. for review. The quality and style of such products, as well as of any carton, container or packing or wrapping material, remains Diplomat's responsibility. Failure to reject any product, carton, container, or packing or wrapping within 15 days of receipt of such item or items by KI, Inc. shall be deemed an acceptance of the quality and style of such item or items. After samples have been approved pursuant to this paragraph, Diplomat shall not depart therefrom in any material respect without KI, Inc.'s prior written consent. From time to time after Diplomat has commenced selling the products, and upon KI, Inc.'s written request, Diplomat shall furnish without cost to KI, Inc. not more than ten (10) additional random samples of each product being manufactured and sold by Diplomat under this Agreement, together with any containers and packing and wrapping material used in connection with such products.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction within Section 9 regarding the 'Quality of Merchandise'. The original text mandates specific standards and requires KI, Inc.'s approval. The modified text weakens these requirements using terms like 'generally align,' 'should be generally,' and makes sample submission 'not mandatory'. However, it maintains the clause about failure to reject resulting in deemed acceptance. This creates an in-text contradiction where the initial part suggests flexibility in standards while the acceptance clause implies strict adherence if samples are submitted, making enforcement unclear. It is not clear if it really mandatory or not, so there may be discrepancies when executing it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Diplomat agrees that the products covered by this Agreement should generally align with a style, appearance, and quality suitable for exploiting KI's name. Diplomat agrees that (a) such products will generally be manufactured, sold, and distributed in accordance with applicable laws, (b) that the policy of sale, distribution, and/or exploitation by Diplomat should be generally to the best advantage of KI, Inc. and KI, and (c) that the latter policy should generally not reflect adversely upon the good name of KI and KI, Inc. To this end, while not mandatory, Diplomat may furnish samples of each product and the cartons, containers, packing and wrapping material for such products to KI, Inc. for review. The quality and style of such products, as well as of any carton, container or packing or wrapping material, remains Diplomat's responsibility. Failure to reject any product, carton, container, or packing or wrapping within 15 days of receipt of such item or items by KI, Inc. shall be deemed an acceptance of the quality and style of such item or items. After samples have been approved pursuant to this paragraph, Diplomat shall not depart therefrom in any material respect without KI, Inc.'s prior written consent. From time to time after Diplomat has commenced selling the products, and upon KI, Inc.'s written request, Diplomat shall furnish without cost to KI, Inc. not more than ten (10) additional random samples of each product being manufactured and sold by Diplomat under this Agreement, together with any containers and packing and wrapping material used in connection with such products.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces ambiguity and weakens the quality control standards ('generally align', 'should be generally', 'not mandatory'), while simultaneously maintaining a clause that deems acceptance of quality if KI, Inc. doesn't reject within 15 days. This creates a direct contradiction. The 'quality and style of such products...remains Diplomat's responsibility' clause is also contradictory in light of the 15 day acceptance clause, suggesting KI, Inc. has no say despite the risk they bear from low quality products. This makes enforcement and quality control unclear and creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Diplomat acknowledges that (except as otherwise provided herein) its failure to commence in good faith to manufacture and distribute in commercial quantities any one or more of the products listed in subparagraph 2(a) within three (3) months of the date of this Agreement and to continue during the term hereof to diligently and continuously manufacture, distribute and sell the products covered by this Agreement, or any class or category thereof, will result in immediate damages to KI, Inc.
perturbation.changed_text: Diplomat acknowledges that (except as otherwise provided herein) its failure to commence in good faith to manufacture and distribute in commercial quantities any one or more of the products listed in subparagraph 2(a) within a reasonable time of the date of this Agreement and to generally continue during the term hereof to manufacture, distribute and sell the products covered by this Agreement, or any class or category thereof, may result in damages to KI, Inc., subject to proof of causation.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction in Section 18(a) regarding remedies for non-performance. Initially, it states that failure to commence manufacturing within three months results in 'immediate damages'. The modified text changes 'three months' to 'a reasonable time,' replaces 'diligently and continuously' with 'generally continue,' and adds 'may result in damages, subject to proof of causation.' This creates ambiguity because the original wording provides a clear, objective trigger for damages, while the modified version requires proving reasonableness, continuity, and direct causation, thereby weakening KI, Inc.'s position and creating an in-text contradiction with KI,Inc's remedies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Diplomat also acknowledges that (except as otherwise provided herein) its failure to cease the manufacture, sale or distribution of the products covered by this Agreement, or any class or category thereof, at the termination or expiration of this Agreement will result in immediate and irremediable damage to KI, Inc. and to the rights of any subsequent licensee. Diplomat acknowledges and admits that there is no adequate remedy at law for such failure to cease manufacture, sale or distribution, and Diplomat agrees that in the event of such failure KI, Inc. shall be entitled to equitable relief by way of temporary and permanent injunctions and such other further relief as any court with jurisdiction may deem just and proper.
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a strong contradiction. The original text stipulated 'immediate damages' for failure to commence manufacturing within three months. The changed text replaces this with 'may result in damages, subject to proof of causation' and changes the 'three months' to 'a reasonable time.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text which states that failure to cease manufacture/sale upon termination will result in 'immediate and irremediable damage'. The initial version sets a clear objective standard for damages, while the modified version introduces uncertainty and requires proof of causation, conflicting with the 'immediate and irremediable damage' clause elsewhere in the agreement.
